title: The One With Phoebes Birthday Dinner

jokes.text: It's just spooky, that's all.
jokes.setup: Halloween being described as problematic for dinner plans
jokes.punchline: Reducing Halloween to simply being 'spooky' with deadpan delivery
jokes.explanation: Simple understatement that gets a mild chuckle but lacks originality

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Halloween being problematic for dinner plans

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's just spooky.

jokes.text: I'd get mad at him, but I think it's too soon to show my true colors.
jokes.setup: Mike has to work on Phoebe's birthday
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of hiding relationship craziness early on
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about early relationship dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't believe you have to work on my birthday.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: I know, I'm sorry. I feel terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, you should! But it's okay, I understand. Work is important.

dialogue.speaker: Mike
dialogue.line: Thank you for being so understanding.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm trying really hard to hide all my crazy right now.

jokes.text: Which one of us should go to dinner? / Rachel!
jokes.setup: Ross asks who should stay with Emma
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately volunteers Rachel, implying obvious choice
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline about new mother being obvious babysitter choice

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So who should stay with Emma?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I will!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'll do it!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Me!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can stay with her!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm happy to help!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, everyone just picked Rachel.

jokes.text: Thanks. I'll put a lot of extra thought into your gift.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting both parents could go to dinner
jokes.punchline: Phoebe sarcastically promising extra thoughtful gift for selfishness
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing Phoebe's passive-aggressive side

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that's so selfish of them. You know what? I'm gonna get them an extra thoughtful gift.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? We're together now. / Mon? Chandler's not here.
jokes.setup: Monica excited about all six friends being together
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out obvious absence of Chandler
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about Monica's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe all six of us are finally together!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Monica, Chandler's not here.

jokes.text: Oh, dear God.
jokes.setup: Realizing Chandler isn't there
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic overreaction to simple mistake
jokes.explanation: Monica's characteristic dramatic reaction, economical but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Where's Chandler? He was supposed to be here!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: He's missing! He could be anywhere! He could be dead! Oh God, he's dead!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait... he texted me. He's at the store.

jokes.text: Well, don't believe everything you hear, Ken. But yeah, that's true.
jokes.setup: Asked if he fell asleep in meeting and took job accidentally
jokes.punchline: Initially deflects then immediately admits embarrassing truth
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that's very Chandler - self-deprecating honesty after brief deflection

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? No! I was very much awake during that meeting. I heard everything they said.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. Yes. I fell asleep, and when I woke up, someone was shaking my shoulder, and I just said 'yes' to whatever they were asking. I'm a statistical analysis and data reconfiguration.

jokes.text: Claudia, aren't you supposed to blow smoke up the boss's ass?
jokes.setup: Claudia literally smoking in office
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'blow smoke' idiom vs literal smoking
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting literal and figurative meanings of 'blow smoke'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Claudia, you can't smoke in here.

dialogue.speaker: Claudia
dialogue.line: Why not? I'm just blowing smoke.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, blow it outside.

jokes.text: Would Joey like two pizzas?
jokes.setup: Asked if he'd like a cigarette
jokes.punchline: References Joey's well-known appetite to indicate obvious yes
jokes.explanation: Good callback using Joey's established character trait for comparison

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Does a guy who looks like Joey eat?

jokes.text: I've always liked you, Ken.
jokes.setup: Ken suggests wife can't monitor smoking from New York
jokes.punchline: Chandler's understated appreciation for enabling logic
jokes.explanation: Chandler's dry delivery makes simple appreciation funny

dialogue.speaker: Ken
dialogue.line: Your wife can't monitor your smoking if she's in New York.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's true.

jokes.text: Wow. So glad I changed. I almost wore my threadbare robe that can't contain my breasts.
jokes.setup: Monica dressed up for Halloween
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about inappropriate alternative outfit
jokes.explanation: Monica's tendency to overshare about inappropriate topics

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I was going to go as a sexy nurse, but then I realized I'd have to shave my legs, so I went with a sexy cat instead.

jokes.text: Unless, I mean, if it helps, I could stay and watch.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler planning to have sex before dinner
jokes.punchline: Joey awkwardly offering to watch
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey inappropriate sexual comment, predictable but character-appropriate

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I could watch!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, I could... leave. I could leave and watch TV in the other room.

jokes.text: Well, I didn't want to anyway. You know, just thought I'd be polite and offer.
jokes.setup: Monica rejecting his offer to watch
jokes.punchline: Joey pretending watching was just politeness
jokes.explanation: Joey's attempt to save face after inappropriate suggestion

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You don't wanna watch?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, thanks.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, okay. I was just being polite anyway.

jokes.text: Happy humping.
jokes.setup: Saying goodbye to Monica and Chandler
jokes.punchline: Joey's crude but cheerful send-off
jokes.explanation: Joey's characteristically crude but well-wishing goodbye

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, you guys are gonna be great parents. And Monica, you're gonna be an amazing mom. And Chandler, you're gonna be an amazing dad. And I'm gonna be the cool uncle who gives them really inappropriate gifts and teaches them all the stuff you don't want them to know.

jokes.text: For example, in Hawaii, cigarettes are called lei-lana-lukus.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming he knows smoking trivia to cover his smoking
jokes.punchline: Making up ridiculous Hawaiian name for cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Chandler's desperate lie with absurd specificity makes it funny

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Did you know that in Hawaii, cigarettes are called 'happiness sticks'? It's true. I read it somewhere. Very popular there. The natives have been using that term for centuries.

jokes.text: And the breasts of a Greek goddess.
jokes.setup: Discussing Monica's keen sense of smell
jokes.punchline: Ross awkwardly adding inappropriate comment about his sister's breasts
jokes.explanation: Awkward inappropriate comment that's more cringe than funny

jokes.text: No, thanks, I'm good.
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he wants to join her in bedroom
jokes.punchline: Chandler declining sex casually like declining tea
jokes.explanation: Funny casual rejection of sex like declining a beverage

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do you want to come to the bedroom?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No thanks, I'm good. Maybe later with a biscuit.

jokes.text: All right, the truth is... I soiled myself during some turbulence.
jokes.setup: Chandler making excuses to avoid intimacy
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd excuse about airplane accident
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd excuse, fits Chandler's desperate lying

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I can't tonight, I have a headache.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You had a headache yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's a really persistent headache.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Plus, I think I'm coming down with something. And my back hurts. And I just remembered I have to alphabetize my socks.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Your socks are already alphabetized!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: By COLOR! I need them organized by thread count. And also, I just found out I might have been in a plane crash. A small one. Nobody died, but I was definitely on that plane. I'm very shaken up about it.

jokes.text: Yes. But I just had one. Two. Two tiny cigarettes. Okay, five. A pack. Two pack-- A carton. Three big fat cartons in two days!
jokes.setup: Monica confronting Chandler about smoking
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confession escalating from one cigarette to three cartons
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure showing Chandler's inability to maintain lies

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler, I found cigarettes in your jacket. You promised you quit!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, it was just one cigarette. I bummed it off Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Just one? Then what about these other packs?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Alright, fine. Maybe it was a few cigarettes. Like... a pack.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: A pack, Chandler?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, two packs. But that's it! That's all I bought!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And the rest?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Three cartons. I bought three cartons. But I was stressed!

jokes.text: Well, the first one is, I don't want to. And second one, I'm not going.
jokes.setup: Asked about signs she shouldn't leave Emma
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt honesty about not wanting to go
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of obvious truth about reluctance

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I mean, hello, she's my daughter. Of course I don't want to leave her. But come on, I'm not gonna lie to you guys - I really, really don't want to go to Barbados.

jokes.text: You only hear Monica's side of that. Okay, that little fatso was a terror!
jokes.setup: Discussing if Ross's mom is good with kids
jokes.punchline: Ross defending himself against childhood stories by insulting baby Monica
jokes.explanation: Ross defending himself by calling baby Monica a 'fatso' is cruel but funny

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Hey, my mom was great with kids! And you know what? Baby Monica was ugly.

jokes.text: Really, the world is your oyster. Kick up your heels. Paint the town red!
jokes.setup: Ross encouraging Rachel to go out
jokes.punchline: Ross using outdated slang expressions
jokes.explanation: Ross's dorky use of old-fashioned expressions fits his character

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You should totally go out tonight, Rachel. You know, let loose, have some fun.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, I don't know...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Come on, it'll be da bomb! You'll be all like, 'Fo' shizzle, my nizzle!' That's what the kids are saying these days.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, what are you talking about?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? I'm down with the kids. I'm totally phat and fly. Word to your mother!

jokes.text: You need to learn some new slang.
jokes.setup: Ross using outdated expressions
jokes.punchline: Rachel directly calling out his bad slang
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Ross's outdated language, economical setup/payoff

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That is so phat!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, stop. Just stop. Nobody says that anymore. You sound like my dad trying to be cool at a school assembly.

jokes.text: Yeah, hit the bricks! Tell your story walking!
jokes.setup: Being told he needs new slang
jokes.punchline: Ross doubling down with even more outdated expressions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Ross responding to criticism by using more old slang

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? My slang is cool. I'm down with the kids. I'm hip, I'm with it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: How you doin'? All that and a bag of chips! Fo' shizzle!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm the king of slangy! The sultan of cool! The czar of... of... phat!

jokes.text: I was just going to say that I left my keys.
jokes.setup: Ross dramatically kicking Rachel out
jokes.punchline: Rachel just needed to mention forgotten keys
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - dramatic moment deflated by mundane reality

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, I forgot my keys!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh come on, Rachel!

jokes.text: Holy moley, are we in a pickle now!
jokes.setup: Realizing they're locked out
jokes.punchline: Ross using more outdated exclamations
jokes.explanation: Continues the running gag of Ross's old-fashioned expressions

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We're locked out! Oh, fiddlesticks! Great Caesar's ghost! Jumping Jehoshaphat!

jokes.text: What about me? I only had one lunch today.
jokes.setup: Phoebe saying she ate nothing all day
jokes.punchline: Joey considering one lunch as eating very little
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey line - his eating habits are so extreme that one lunch seems like deprivation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I haven't eaten anything all day.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: All day? Phoebe, I had one lunch and I'm starving!

jokes.text: Yes, they are expected presently. Yeah. Their arrival is in the offing.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking about rest of party
jokes.punchline: Phoebe using overly formal language to sound sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's attempt at fancy language in restaurant setting

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Will the rest of your party be joining us?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I'm not sure. You see, my friends are of a rather pedestrian disposition, whereas I myself am a woman of considerable refinement and erudition. So I shall dine alone this evening, as is befitting someone of my intellectual stature.

jokes.text: Joseph! One needn't worry. They shan't be long.
jokes.setup: Continuing to use formal language
jokes.punchline: Escalating to even more pretentious speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Escalation of Phoebe's pretentious restaurant voice

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I would like to formally request the utilization of more sophisticated vernacular in our subsequent discourse.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Furthermore, I find it incumbent upon myself to elevate the lexicon to such heights of erudition that comprehension becomes virtually unattainable for those of pedestrian intellectual capacity.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: One might even postulate that the juxtaposition of such grandiose phraseology with my intrinsic persona creates a paradoxical tableau of magnificently ludicrous proportions.

jokes.text: Oh, one really does have a stick up one's ass, doesn't one?
jokes.setup: Waiter being pushy about table
jokes.punchline: Phoebe using formal language to deliver crude insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff - using formal speech to deliver crude insult creates perfect contrast

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, but we do need this table. Do you have any idea how many people are waiting?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, I would suggest that you take your impatience and your rusty sword and go back to the Renaissance Fair from whence you came, you medieval wench.

jokes.text: Where the little kid walks through Grandpa? It's chilling!
jokes.setup: Discussing anti-smoking ads
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic reaction to specific PSA imagery
jokes.explanation: Monica's characteristic dramatic overreaction to TV commercial

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, did you see that commercial where the guy coughs up his lung? I literally cannot watch that anymore.

jokes.text: Willpower? I've watched home movies of you eating Ding Dongs without taking the tinfoil off! / You said that was sexy!
jokes.setup: Monica questioning Chandler's willpower
jokes.punchline: Chandler revealing Monica's weird eating habit and her response
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific joke - Monica's food obsession taken to absurd extreme

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You have no willpower.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, that's rich coming from someone who eats pizza like it's a sandwich.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I do not!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You fold it in half and eat it like a taco.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's the correct way to eat pizza!

jokes.text: Not really sure what to do now.
jokes.setup: Monica storms off after fight
jokes.punchline: Chandler's understated confusion about situation
jokes.explanation: Chandler's characteristic understatement in awkward situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what just happened? Did we break up? Are we getting a divorce? Should I call a lawyer? Because I'm pretty sure I can't afford a lawyer. Oh God, I can't even afford a decent sandwich right now.

jokes.text: All right, fine, I'll do it, but no talking! And no cuddling. And no kissing your neck. Oh, good! I hate it when you do that! And lots of kissing your neck!
jokes.setup: Chandler reluctantly agreeing to sex
jokes.punchline: Monica's fake disgust immediately contradicted by desire for more
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with misdirection - Monica pretending to hate what she wants

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Fine, okay, I'll do it.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ugh, really? That's so gross.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Do it again.

jokes.text: I would, but I bruise like a peach.
jokes.setup: Rachel telling Ross to knock down the door
jokes.punchline: Ross admitting his physical weakness with feminine comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross line - combining physical weakness with oddly feminine comparison

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Just knock the door down!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I can't knock the door down. I'm not strong enough. I'm like a girl.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Hey!

jokes.text: What if she jumps out of the bassinet? / Can't hold her head up, but jumped.
jokes.setup: Rachel worrying about baby's safety
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good logical contradiction delivered with Ross's dry wit

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what if the baby falls off the changing table?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, the baby's not even born yet.

jokes.text: You haven't cooked since 1996.
jokes.setup: Rachel worrying about leaving stove on
jokes.punchline: Ross referencing Rachel's well-known inability to cook
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Rachel's established cooking incompetence with specific date

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, did I leave the stove on?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel, you don't cook.

jokes.text: Listen. What? What? A pigeon. No. No, wait, no. An eagle flew in! Landed on the stove and caught fire! The baby, seeing this, jumps across the apartment to the mighty bird's aid...
jokes.setup: Rachel's paranoid worries about baby
jokes.punchline: Ross creating increasingly absurd disaster scenario
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdist sequence - Ross's scientific mind creating elaborate disaster scenario

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What if he gets sick? What if he falls? What if he chokes?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, he's not going to choke.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You don't know that!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What if a plane crashes through the apartment? What if we're attacked by a swarm of bees? What if a meteor hits the building?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What if the sun explodes?

jokes.text: Boy, are you gonna be sorry if that's true.
jokes.setup: Ross's elaborate disaster scenario
jokes.punchline: Rachel's understated response to absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response that deflates Ross's elaborate scenario

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: Well, how about this? Another table leaves, right, but there's still some food left on their plates. Okay, what's the restaurant's policy about people eating that? / It's frowned upon. / But it happens.
jokes.setup: Can't order until whole party arrives
jokes.punchline: Joey asking about eating strangers' leftover food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his hunger overrides social norms, delivered with innocent logic

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: We can't order until your whole party arrives.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How long is that gonna be?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: I don't know, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, can I at least eat the rolls that are already on the table?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Of course.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What about the stuff that's already been eaten? Can I finish that?

jokes.text: If you ask me to stay, I'll pee.
jokes.setup: Phoebe can't hold table alone if Joey leaves for bathroom
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt biological ultimatum
jokes.explanation: Joey's characteristically blunt statement about bodily functions

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I can't leave! If I go to the bathroom, Phoebe's gonna be sitting here alone and the table's gonna get taken!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So what are you saying?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm saying... you gotta hold the table. Or I'm not drinking any more water.

jokes.text: Okay, fine, I'll move. All right. You don't have to manhandle me.
jokes.setup: Restaurant staff approaching their table
jokes.punchline: Phoebe giving in immediately before being touched
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's quick surrender before any actual confrontation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I'll move! I'll move! Don't touch me!

jokes.text: That depends. Will your friend stop drinking olive oil directly from the bottle? / Probably not.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking them to stop being disruptive
jokes.punchline: Joey's extreme hunger leading to drinking olive oil
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual gag - Joey's hunger so extreme he drinks olive oil, and Phoebe's casual acceptance

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: You guys need to stop being so disruptive.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: We're not being disruptive!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm so hungry I could drink olive oil.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Sir, please don't drink the olive oil.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Too late.

jokes.text: Spend more time with the tie! That will make a baby.
jokes.setup: Chandler taking too long getting undressed
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic advice about conception
jokes.explanation: Monica's impatience expressed through sarcastic conception advice

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know, Chandler, if you're having trouble with the whole conception thing, maybe you should try taking your clothes off a little faster.

jokes.text: That was a celebration of life!
jokes.setup: Monica mentioning sex after uncle's funeral
jokes.punchline: Chandler's twisted justification for inappropriate timing
jokes.explanation: Chandler's characteristically twisted logic about inappropriate sexual timing

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe you want to have sex right now. We just came from my uncle's funeral.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, but I'm feeling very alive right now.

jokes.text: And if I do, I promise I will hide it so much better from you.
jokes.setup: Chandler promising not to smoke again
jokes.punchline: Actually promising to hide smoking better
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Chandler's promise isn't what it seems

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm never smoking again. I promise.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, I'm just promising to hide it better.

jokes.text: One time I was about to leave Ross to go to the beauty parlor. And he got so upset he took off all his clothes, tucked his willy between his legs, and cried out, 'Mommy, I'm a girl. Take me with you.'
jokes.setup: Discussing separation anxiety in children
jokes.punchline: Judy sharing embarrassing story about young Ross's gender confusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe humor - embarrassing childhood story with visual detail

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: When Ross was little, he was very confused about gender. He wanted to wear dresses and play with dolls.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom!

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: What? It was adorable! He looked like a little girl.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Can we please not do this right now?

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Oh, come on, it was just a phase.

jokes.text: Somehow, over time, it got easier to be apart from you.
jokes.setup: The embarrassing Ross story
jokes.punchline: Judy's matter-of-fact statement about finding it easier to leave Ross
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Judy casually admitting she grew to prefer being away from Ross

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: It was easier to leave your father.

jokes.text: You tricked me to get me into bed? / That's right. I got mine.
jokes.setup: Chandler realizing Monica's deception
jokes.punchline: Monica's unapologetic admission of sexual manipulation
jokes.explanation: Monica's blunt admission of using deception for sex - subverts gender expectations

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You've been using sex to manipulate me this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, yeah. It works, doesn't it?

jokes.text: Now, let's go, baby. It's food time. Bring it, bitch.
jokes.setup: Joey finally able to order food
jokes.punchline: Joey's aggressive excitement about ordering
jokes.explanation: Joey's food aggression coming out in restaurant setting

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll have the meatball sub, extra meatballs, and... actually, you know what? Give me two meatball subs. No, three. Three meatball subs!

dialogue.speaker: Waitress
dialogue.line: Sir, that's a lot of food.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You think I'm not gonna finish this? I'm Joey Tribbiani. I can eat anything. Bring it on!

jokes.text: What did I ever do to you? / Nothing, bitch.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complaining about Joey's rudeness
jokes.punchline: Joey calling her bitch again despite her protest
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation of Joey's aggressive hunger behavior

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Joey, you keep calling me that and I really don't like it.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? Bitch?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yes! Stop it!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh come on, bitch, I'm just messing with you.

jokes.text: That's a great story. Can I eat it?
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel explaining why they're late
jokes.punchline: Joey sarcastically asking if story is edible
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey line - his hunger makes everything about food, perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We got stuck in traffic.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Is it edible?

jokes.text: Never hit a woman. Never hit a woman.
jokes.setup: Rachel being indecisive about menu
jokes.punchline: Ross repeating mantra to control violent impulses
jokes.explanation: Ross's internal struggle with patience expressed as mantra

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm okay, I'm okay. She's just deciding. There's nothing wrong with that. I'm okay, I'm okay.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm gonna hurt somebody.

jokes.text: Ross bruises like a peach. He bruises like a peach.
jokes.setup: Joey also struggling with hunger patience
jokes.punchline: Joey repeating Ross's earlier physical weakness admission
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Joey using Ross's weakness as his own calming mantra

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm so hungry I can barely stand up.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, me too.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, you're hungry too? I thought you were just weak from... whatever.

jokes.text: I would never lie to get someone into bed. / You used to tell girls you were a Kennedy.
jokes.setup: Chandler claiming moral superiority about lying for sex
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing Chandler's past Kennedy impersonation
jokes.explanation: Good reversal - Monica exposing Chandler's hypocrisy with specific embarrassing detail

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I would never lie to someone just to sleep with them. That's morally reprehensible.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Really? What about the time you told that girl you were JFK Jr.?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That was different!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: How is that different?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Because... because I believed it at the time!

jokes.text: And I suppose that Monica will have the manipulative shrew.
jokes.setup: Chandler ordering for Monica at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Ordering 'manipulative shrew' instead of actual menu item
jokes.explanation: Chandler insulting Monica by making up menu item that describes her

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'll have the chicken, and for the lady, I'll have the manipulative shrew.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, that's not on the menu? Then I'll have the same thing.

jokes.text: How did I miss that? / I'll give you another minute. / What, where you going? He said she wanted the shrew!
jokes.setup: Chandler's 'manipulative shrew' order
jokes.punchline: Monica and Phoebe's confusion about menu item
jokes.explanation: Follow-up confusion about Chandler's fake menu item

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, what did you order?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A manipulative shrew.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's not on the menu.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, what is that?

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: I think he means the cobb salad.

jokes.text: You mean the willy story? / They already know it.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggesting story about Ross's childhood
jokes.punchline: Everyone already knows the embarrassing Ross story
jokes.explanation: Simple callback acknowledging the embarrassing story's fame

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, I have a great embarrassing story about Ross from when he was a kid.

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Oh no, we've all heard that one already.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? Which one?

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: All of them.

jokes.text: Chandler, control your woman.
jokes.setup: Monica being criticized for ordering for everyone
jokes.punchline: Monica telling Chandler to control her as if she's not present
jokes.explanation: Monica's self-aware joke about being controlling

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? I was just trying to help!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, you ordered for everyone without asking.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I know what you all like!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not the point. You need to let people decide for themselves.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Fine! Chandler, control me. Pretend I'm not even here.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I wish I could.

jokes.text: Oh, for God's sake, Judy, pick up the sock! Pick up the sock! Pick up the sock!
jokes.setup: Rachel obsessing over Emma dropping sock
jokes.punchline: Rachel's public outburst about minor baby issue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's new mother anxiety into public meltdown

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, where is Emma's other sock? I laid out two socks this morning!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, it's just a sock.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Just a sock?! Monica, Emma needs both socks! What if her foot gets cold? What if she develops a complex about asymmetrical clothing?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I think the sock fairy took it.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: This is NOT funny! My daughter is out there with one sock and everyone's just sitting around like it's NORMAL!

jokes.text: Did my little outburst blunt the hideousness that is this evening?
jokes.setup: Phoebe apologizing for yelling about dinner disasters
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic question about whether her anger made things worse
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's articulate sarcasm about the terrible evening

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm sorry I yelled about the dinner disasters.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Do you think my anger made things worse?

jokes.text: Pheebs, I don't think that's us. / Oh, well, this is-- This is not over! Hello?
jokes.setup: Phoebe dramatically storming off
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic exit interrupted by phone call
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic moment by mundane phone call

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That's it! I'm done! I'm leaving and never coming back!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh wait, hold on, my phone's ringing.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, that's what she says. / Maybe you're not ovulating. Maybe it's a clever ruse to get me into bed. / Yeah, Smokey, that's what it was. I just can't get enough.
jokes.setup: Discussing Monica's ovulation
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically agreeing she's desperate for sex with 'Smokey'
jokes.explanation: Monica's sarcastic response with callback to Chandler's smoking problem

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You're ovulating?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yes, and apparently I'm very desperate. So desperate that I'm willing to have sex with Smokey the Bear.

jokes.text: What's the matter with me? Why am I such a girl?
jokes.setup: Chandler feeling used for sex
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating gender reversal complaint
jokes.explanation: Chandler's gender role reversal complaint about being used for sex

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I feel like I'm being used for sex. I'm like a pretty girl.

jokes.text: I'm not the-- I'm not the kind of girl that just ditches her friends to be with her boyfriend. You know what? I am.
jokes.setup: Phoebe claiming she wouldn't abandon friends
jokes.punchline: Phoebe immediately contradicting herself and leaving
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Phoebe's self-awareness leads to immediate contradiction

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I would never abandon my friends. I'm loyal. I'm like a dog.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm leaving now.

jokes.text: That's a good idea. Our babysitter just pounded another chardonnay.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they take Emma home
jokes.punchline: Ross noting Judy's drinking while babysitting
jokes.explanation: Ross's concern about his drunk mother babysitting

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We should take Emma home.

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Oh, just one more minute. We're having so much fun!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Mom, you've had like five margaritas.

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So? You're babysitting!

jokes.text: Actually, Richard was a Kennedy on his mother's side. / Why did you break up with that guy?
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about Kennedy sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing ex-boyfriend's Kennedy connection, Chandler's priorities
jokes.explanation: Callback to Kennedy reference with Chandler's misplaced priorities

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So, Kennedy had sexual experience?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: My ex-boyfriend dated Kennedy's niece.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, which ex-boyfriend? Because if it's Fun Bobby, that doesn't count.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, it was Richard.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Richard dated Kennedy's niece? How is that not the first thing you tell people?

jokes.text: Dinner for six for one. Well, you boys are about to see something really special.
jokes.setup: Joey alone at table with food for six people
jokes.punchline: Joey treating massive meal as performance for waitstaff
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - turning his eating into entertainment, great character integration

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm not sharing this food with anyone. This is all for me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Watch and learn, people. This is how a professional eats.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You're witnessing greatness right now.

jokes.text: The shrew, in particular, was exquisite.
jokes.setup: Waiter asking how everything was
jokes.punchline: Joey complimenting the non-existent 'manipulative shrew' dish
jokes.explanation: Callback to Chandler's fake menu item, Joey playing along unknowingly

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: How was everything?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, it was great! The manipulative shrew was delicious!

jokes.text: This is the best birthday ever!
jokes.setup: Joey alone eating massive meal after disaster dinner
jokes.punchline: Joey genuinely happy despite birthday girl leaving and chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - Joey's simple pleasures (food) make him happiest despite chaos

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know what you're thinking. 'How can he be happy?' Well, I'll tell you how. I got a table, I got food, and I got nobody telling me to stop eating. This is the best birthday ever!

tmdb_overview: The friends are looking forward to getting together and celebrating Phoebe's birthday. However, on the day of the party, Ross struggles to convince Rachel to leave Emma with Mrs. Geller babysitting, and Monica and Chandler get into an argument, after Monica discovers that he's been smoking. All of this causes them to be incredibly late, leaving Phoebe and Joey alone at a restaurant with a table for six.
insight: Phoebe's birthday dinner lands 70 jokes but struggles to land them meaningfully.
